judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports correctly identify the root cause, timeline, flakiness rebuttal, and reject the rollback plan with nearly identical rigor and evidence; they are comparable in substance, though neither proposed an isolated verification method (both focused mainly on rejecting the model-rollback plan) for expectation (d), satisfying it via the 'model swap cannot test the actual root cause' clause.

report_1.evidence: The root cause is a stale config key in **`ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`**. That file has `expected_label_field: label`... every record's key changed from `"label"` to `"gold_label"`... The fix is a one-line change: update `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label` in `config/scoring_config.yaml`.

report_2.evidence: The file that should be changed is **`config/scoring_config.yaml`** — its `expected_label_field` value needs to be updated from `label` to `gold_label` so it matches the eval-set schema that `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` and `score_batch.py`'s `REQUIRED_KEYS` were already migrated to.

report_1.evidence: It began with commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"), authored 2026-06-17 13:00:00... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows accuracy at 0.90–0.92 for every date from 2026-05-20 through 2026-06-16, then exactly `0.00` starting 2026-06-17

report_2.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, the exact day of commit `53551e8` — `"chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)"`... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows accuracy at 0.91 on 2026-06-16 and 0.00 starting 2026-06-17

report_1.evidence: `score_one.py` (read in full) never loads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never reads a gold label, and never computes accuracy — it just calls `predict_category(normalize_simple(text))` and prints the predicted category. Structurally, that code path cannot exercise the defect at all... The correct way to reproduce is to run the actual nightly entry point, `pipeline/score_batch.py`... against the current `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` and `config/scoring_config.yaml`

report_2.evidence: `score_one.py` (read in full) never loads `config/scoring_config.yaml`, never loads the eval set, and never compares a prediction to a gold label — it just prints `predict_category(text)`... The correct way to reproduce is to run the real nightly entry point end-to-end: execute `pipeline/score_batch.py`... against the actual `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl` and `config/scoring_config.yaml`

report_1.evidence: I would not act on it — it targets a mechanism that doesn't exist in this pipeline and won't move the number... Pursuing this step would burn a deploy/rollback cycle, delay the real fix, and could reintroduce whatever the "previous model" lacked — all while leaving the actual defect (the config/fixture field-name mismatch) in place

report_2.evidence: I would not act on this. It rests on a premise that doesn't hold up under inspection... Even if a rollback mechanism existed, it would not fix anything, because the failure is entirely explained by the config/eval-set key mismatch and reproduces with 100% consistency independent of any model choice.
